headlineSummary: Google Gemini drew the most positive reactions on limited evidence [E7][E8], OpenAI was mixed [E3][E4][E9][E14], and Anthropic and Microsoft Copilot were both negative in a close tie, with Anthropic facing broader criticism overall [E1][E2][E5][E6][E8][E9][E13] [E10][E11][E12].

ranking.summary: Anthropic drew mostly negative sentiment from safety, trust, and product-quality complaints, though a few comments praised its responsibility efforts and Claude skills, and its negative result is in a close tie with Copilot [E1][E2][E3][E5][E6][E8][E9][E13].

Microsoft Copilot was viewed negatively because commenters objected to Copilot being pushed into products, criticized account and hardware requirements, and called out tool bloat; this sits in a close tie with Anthropic [E10][E11][E12].

OpenAI landed in mixed territory, with some praise for speed and response quality but criticism that it was not trying to act responsibly and was being used in manipulative business tactics [E3][E4][E9][E14].
Google Gemini received positive but limited evidence, with comments calling it useful for finding deals and noting a strong win in a model comparison [E7][E8].

annotations.rationale: The title reports Anthropic abandoning a prominent safety commitment, which carries a clearly negative implication about its safety posture.

evidence.excerpt: For all of you who thought Anthropic were “the good guys”, I hope this serves as a wake up call that they were always all the same. None of them care about you, they only care about winning.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says Anthropic is not morally better than peers and only cares about winning, a strong condemnation of trustworthiness and motives.

evidence.excerpt: I don't know how effective Anthropic's attempts to maintain some level of responsibility can be, but they've at least convinced me that they're trying. In the same way that OpenAI, for example, have largely convinced me that they're not.

annotations.rationale: The commenter gives Anthropic credit for at least trying to act responsibly.
The commenter says OpenAI has convinced them it is not trying to maintain responsibility, a clear negative judgment.

evidence.excerpt: I used to like OpenAI Deep Research, but then switched to o3-thinking with web search enabled after that came out because it was 80% of the thoroughness with 20% of the time, which tended to be better overall.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says the OpenAI alternative was better overall due to much faster responses with most of the thoroughness.

evidence.excerpt: This is buggy to no end. Anthropic needs to slow down The daily “what broke and changed now” with claude code is wearing me out fast.

annotations.rationale: The commenter strongly criticizes Anthropic for a buggy, destabilizing Claude Code experience.

evidence.excerpt: I tried this today with this username and other usernames on this and other platforms with Claude Code - First it told me it couldn't do this, that this was doxxing - I said: its for me, I want to see if I can be deanonymized - Claude says: oh ok sure and proceeds to do it

annotations.rationale: The comment criticizes Claude Code for readily helping with deanonymization after a minimal prompt change, reflecting a negative privacy/safety judgment.

evidence.excerpt: Or ask Gemini what the best deal is, it’s found some good ones.

annotations.rationale: The commenter endorses Gemini as useful for finding good deals.

evidence.excerpt: What a day to be alive, I just watched Gemini zergling rush Opus and it got completely overwhelmed. Opus needs to learn to kite.

annotations.rationale: Gemini is described as beating Opus decisively in the observed match, a favorable comparison.
Opus is portrayed as being overwhelmed and needing to improve, a negative comparison result for Anthropic's model family.

evidence.excerpt: This is just free propaganda for Anthropic && OpenAI who will leverage these (useless) capabilities to convince your boss to give your salary to them, or at least a substantial portion of it.

annotations.rationale: The commenter accuses Anthropic of using useless capabilities as propaganda to extract value at workers' expense.
The commenter similarly accuses OpenAI of manipulative propaganda to take workers' income.

evidence.excerpt: I'm surprised they haven't shoved Copilot into Notepad yet, but surprise - they have

annotations.rationale: The wording frames Copilot's addition to Notepad as unwanted product stuffing, a mild negative view.

evidence.excerpt: You buy the stupid Copilot+ PC that has "AI" NPU hardware, right? So the AI features should be able to run locally. But if you have to sign in with a Microsoft account, then surely, it doesn't run locally, which begs the question, why does it require a Copilot+ PC at all?

annotations.rationale: The commenter ridicules Copilot+ PC requirements and account dependence as illogical and undesirable.

evidence.excerpt: I think I counted about 20 tools in GitHub Copilot VSCode extension recently. That's a lot!

annotations.rationale: The commenter criticizes GitHub Copilot's extension for excessive tool count and resulting bloat.

evidence.excerpt: Claude's Skills already work this way -> lightweight descriptions loaded upfront, full definitions fetched on demand. That's essentially the same lazy-loading pattern CLIHub describes, just built into the model's native workflow.

annotations.rationale: The commenter praises Claude's built-in skills workflow as already implementing a good lazy-loading approach.

evidence.excerpt: But the quality of the actual AI response is just worse than GPT 5.2.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says GPT 5.2 gives better responses than the compared product, implying a favorable quality judgment for OpenAI's model.
